_comment: Compact plan authored by the LLM from ONE pass over the source document. Everything else is generated from this file.

<flow_key>.canonical_flow_key: <optional: another flow_key in this map to emit instead (e.g. anhydrous form)>

processes.reference_output_flow: <flow_key that is the reference product OUT>

inputs.comment: <optional audit note carried into the ILCD exchange comment>

outputs.comment: <optional audit note carried into the ILCD exchange comment>

_rules: Use the SAME flow_key across processes to create edges (upstream OUTPUT key == downstream INPUT key).
Every process must have exactly one output whose flow equals reference_output_flow; that is the reference exchange.
Amounts are per 1 unit of the process's reference output.

derivation=Measured: the source text gives this exact quantity. derivation=Calculated: derived from source-given molar ratios/formulas/recipe fractions — MUST supply calc_note. derivation=Estimated: defaulted from engineering models (electricity/water/O2/waste should come from estimate-utilities.mjs).
For Estimated electricity/water/O2/waste exchanges, copy the estimator formula_ref and any source_ref into the exchange; materialize-from-plan carries those fields into ILCD generalComment.

If a process is black_box=true because material quantities are not defensible, every flow used by that process must use unit=item and every exchange amount must be 1. Downstream processes consuming an item-unit flow inherit that item-based treatment.
pure_oxygen=true is required to declare any O2 input (flow key containing 'o2' or 'oxygen'). Only set it when the source explicitly names a pure-O2 atmosphere.

canonical_flow_key lets a flow (e.g. 'coso4_7h2o' hydrate) emit as another flow (e.g. 'coso4' anhydrous) with amount multiplied by conversion_factor (e.g. 154.99/281.10 ≈ 0.551). Both flows must be declared in this flows{} map; the canonical one is the form that actually enters the ILCD dataset.
Omit _comment and _rules in the actual plan; they are illustrative only.
